entry.title: Micron Q3 FY2026 guidance: DDR5 ASP up 40% QoQ; capacity allocation shifting further to HBM3E
entry.summary: Confirms the commodity super-spike is supply-driven, not demand-led. RI implication: R_C continues to rise through Q3; composite RI likely holds above 0.8 through year-end if HBM conversion persists.
entry.content:
Confirms the commodity super-spike is supply-driven, not demand-led. RI implication: R_C continues to rise through Q3; composite RI likely holds above 0.8 through year-end if HBM conversion persists.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Samsung shifts focus from HBM to DDR5 modules: DDR5 RAM results in FAR more profits than HBM
entry.summary: Samsung is reportedly scaling down its HBM production, shifting focus of DRAM production to DDR5 modules because there's FAR more profits to be made.
entry.content:
Samsung is reportedly scaling down its HBM production, shifting focus of DRAM production to DDR5 modules because there's FAR more profits to be made.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: AI memory is sold out, causing an unprecedented surge in prices

entry.title: RAM Prices 2026: Buy Now or Wait as Gartner Forecasts 130% Memory Cost Surge
entry.summary: On June 3, Tom’s Hardware reported that the cheapest 32GB DDR5 kit available in the United States had climbed to $374.97, with no comparable option selling for less, a detail captured in its daily RAM price tracker.
entry.content:
On June 3, Tom’s Hardware reported that the cheapest 32GB DDR5 kit available in the United States had climbed to $374.97, with no comparable option selling for less, a detail captured in its daily RAM price tracker.

entry.title: RAM Is Reportedly Back In Production — But It Still Won't Be Enough
entry.summary: More RAM is coming, but the shortage isn't going away. Even with new production ramping up, the supply problems plaguing PC builders could stretch for years.
entry.content:
More RAM is coming, but the shortage isn't going away. Even with new production ramping up, the supply problems plaguing PC builders could stretch for years.

entry.title: Intel Prepares HBM Killer: HB3DM Memory Stacks with Z-Angle Technology

At VLSI 2026 in June, Saimemory is scheduled to present a pa...
entry.content:

At VLSI 2026 in June, Saimemory is scheduled to present a pa...

entry.title: DDR5 RAM Hits $375 Floor for PC Builders: HBM Takes Three Times More Wafers
entry.summary: DDR5 RAM price 2026 has hit a structural $375 floor for PC builders, confirmed by PCPartPicker data, as AI data centers absorb DRAM wafer capacity at triple the rate of standard memory. Samsung and SK Hynix warn the shortage runs through at least 2027. Here is what builders must budget now — and a
entry.content:
DDR5 RAM price 2026 has hit a structural $375 floor for PC builders, confirmed by PCPartPicker data, as AI data centers absorb DRAM wafer capacity at triple the rate of standard memory. Samsung and SK Hynix warn the shortage runs through at least 2027. Here is what builders must budget now — and a

entry.title: SK Hynix tells investors its US listing plan wins their strong backing — Reuters
entry.summary: SK Hynix told investors this week it received "tremendously positive" feedback on its proposed US listing as the chipmaker benefits from strong demand for advanced memory semiconductors needed to run AI data centres, a source familiar with the matter said on Thursday.
entry.content:
SK Hynix told investors this week it received "tremendously positive" feedback on its proposed US listing as the chipmaker benefits from strong demand for advanced memory semiconductors needed to run AI data centres, a source familiar with the matter said on Thursday.

entry.title: Nvidia Blackwell GB200 NVL72 rack demand accelerating; SK Hynix locked into HBM3E supply through Q4
Samsung delays 1c-nm DRAM ramp by 2 quarters citing yield challenges at Pyeongtaek P4 fab

entry.title: Mind the gap! The semiconductor industry is relying on the wrong materials
entry.summary: 2D materials are widely seen as a promising path toward better computer chips. Researchers at TU Wien have now shown that some of these materials are unsuitable due to an underestimated effect. But there are alternatives.
entry.content:
2D materials are widely seen as a promising path toward better computer chips. Researchers at TU Wien have now shown that some of these materials are unsuitable due to an underestimated effect. But there are alternatives.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: PC DRAM spot prices hit 7-year high; module prices up 180% from October 2024 lows

Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM. But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s! In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project… | Laurie Kirk | 25 comments
entry.summary:
Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM.
But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s!
In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project (Tailslayer) that immensely reduces p99.99 latency on traditional RAM!
By implementing a hedged read strategy taking advantage of (undocumented!) channel scrambling offsets, I've gotten as much as 15x reductions in tail latency.
The technique works across Intel, AMD, Graviton, DDR4, DDR5, x86, ARM, you name it.
Check out the C++ lib I wrote, watch the video, and try it yourself! | 25 comments on LinkedIn
entry.content:
Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM. But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s! In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project (Tailslayer) that immensely reduces p99.99 latency on traditional RAM! By implementing a hedged read strategy taking advantage of (undocumented!) channel scrambling offsets, I've gotten as much as 15x reductions in tail latency. The technique works across Intel, AMD, Graviton, DDR4, DDR5, x86, ARM, you name it. Check out the C++ lib I wrote, watch the video, and try it yourself! | 25 comments on LinkedIn

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM. But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s! In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project… | Laurie Kirk | 25 comments
entry.summary:
Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM.
But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s!
In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project (Tailslayer) that immensely reduces p99.99 latency on traditional RAM!
By implementing a hedged read strategy taking advantage of (undocumented!) channel scrambling offsets, I've gotten as much as 15x reductions in tail latency.
The technique works across Intel, AMD, Graviton, DDR4, DDR5, x86, ARM, you name it.
Check out the C++ lib I wrote, watch the video, and try it yourself! | 25 comments on LinkedIn
entry.content:
Modern DRAM is based on a brilliant design from IBM. But, we're still paying for a latency penalty that's existed since the 60s! In this video, I'm introducing my personal research project (Tailslayer) that immensely reduces p99.99 latency on traditional RAM! By implementing a hedged read strategy taking advantage of (undocumented!) channel scrambling offsets, I've gotten as much as 15x reductions in tail latency. The technique works across Intel, AMD, Graviton, DDR4, DDR5, x86, ARM, you name it. Check out the C++ lib I wrote, watch the video, and try it yourself! | 25 comments on LinkedIn

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: New Cambridge human brain-inspired chip could slash AI energy use — new type of memristor has roughly a million times lower switching current than conventional devices | Tom's Hardware
entry.summary: The devices use a self-assembled p-n junction inside the oxide film instead of conductive filaments.
entry.content:
The devices use a self-assembled p-n junction inside the oxide film instead of conductive filaments.

entry.title: Inverse-designed nanophotonic neural network accelerators for ultra-compact optical computing | Nature Communications
entry.summary: Scaling of optical computing requires computationally dense and efficient hardware. Here, the authors inverse-design and experimentally demonstrate ultracompact nanophotonic neural network accelerators with high computational density, enabling scalable and energy-efficient analog photonic computing.
entry.content:
Scaling of optical computing requires computationally dense and efficient hardware. Here, the authors inverse-design and experimentally demonstrate ultracompact nanophotonic neural network accelerators with high computational density, enabling scalable and energy-efficient analog photonic computing.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.

entry.title: Adeia sues AMD for patent infringement over semiconductor technology | Reuters

entry.content:

entry.title: 3D NAND: The Most Scalable Semiconductor

entry.title: Scientists smash record in stacking semiconductor transistors for large-area electronics
entry.summary: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST; Saudi Arabia) researchers have set a record in microchip design, achieving the first six-stack hybrid CMOS (complementary metal-oxide semiconductor) for large-area electronics. With no other reported hybrid CMOS exceeding two stacks, the feat marks a new benchmark in integration density and efficiency, opening possibilities in electronic miniaturization and performance.
entry.content:
King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST; Saudi Arabia) researchers have set a record in microchip design, achieving the first six-stack hybrid CMOS (complementary metal-oxide semiconductor) for large-area electronics. With no other reported hybrid CMOS exceeding two stacks, the feat marks a new benchmark in integration density and efficiency, opening possibilities in electronic miniaturization and performance.

Editorial pick — curated by The RAM Index.
